name: Yam Gai (Thai Broiled Chicken Salad)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the Wun Sen noodles in hot water for 30 minutes.
2. In a food processor, combine garlic, peppercorns, and 1/4 cup coriander. Blend to a paste.
3. Scrape the sides of the food processor and add 0.25 cups soy sauce and 2 tablespoons fish sauce. Pour the marinade into a shallow pan.
4. Add cubed chicken (about 1 inch) to the marinade. Preheat the broiler.
5. In a medium saucepan, bring the chicken stock to a boil. Drain the soaked noodles and add them to the boiling stock for about 5 minutes. Drain well and cut into 4 pieces.
6. Remove the chicken from the marinade and broil until browned, about 4 to 7 minutes.
7. In a small bowl, stir together sugar, fresh lime juice, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, and 1 tablespoon fish sauce.
8. Line a large platter with lettuce leaves. Spread the noodles over the lettuce and top with tomato wedges, chopped scallions, and diced cucumber.
9. Arrange the broiled chicken on top of the salad and pour the dressing over it. Sprinkle with remaining coriander and peanuts.
10. Garnish with lime slices.
